summary: Approved after Hermes addressed the OpenCode GLM-5.1 review findings that were required for this bounded no-dependency parser slice. The change removes placeholder PDF/ePUB parser output and adds behavior-focused tests without changing provider interfaces, frontend contracts, dependencies, remotes, or secrets.

findings.description: PDF extraction is intentionally limited to simple uncompressed streams and literal strings. Most real PDFs use compression or encodings, so full PDF quality remains a future task.
OCR implementation was split from the original combined parser/OCR backlog item into blg_backend_ocr_provider.

accepted_changes: Added EPUB ZIP/OPF spine parsing with HTML/XHTML fallback, entity decoding, script/style stripping, stable chapter IDs, size limits, and tests.
Added PDF simple uncompressed stream literal-string extraction with escape/octal handling and tests.
Added tests for invalid inputs, nested OPF href paths, sanitized EPUB content, PDF placeholder removal, and parser factory compatibility.

security_review.notes: No secrets or remotes changed. EPUB archive entry names are used as in-memory map keys only; no filesystem extraction is performed. Decompressed size limits reduce zip-bomb risk.

quality_review.notes: No dependency or API scope creep. Standard go command is unavailable on PATH; supplemental parser tests passed via cached Go toolchain path.

validation.notes: go binary not installed on PATH in Hermes environment
Supplemental validation only; not counted as satisfying the standard go-on-PATH prerequisite.
